## Summary

Fixes two production issues:

1. **PDF export failing** (`PDF_EXPORT_FAILED`): `@sparticuz/chromium@149` requires Node `^22.17.0` but Vercel runs `22.11.0`. Downgraded to `@sparticuz/chromium@143.0.4` (Node >= 16) and `puppeteer-core@22.15.0` (Node >= 18).

2. **Market Readiness missing from DOCX/TXT**: The web page and PDF already show Market Readiness score/confidence in the header area, but DOCX and TXT did not. Added to both formats.

## Scope

**Touches:**
- `package.json` / `package-lock.json` — dependency versions
- `app/api/reports/[jobId]/download/route.ts` — DOCX builder (Market Readiness paragraph), TXT builder (Market Readiness line), removed stale `@ts-expect-error`

**Does NOT touch:**
- Web page rendering
- PDF HTML template (already has Market Readiness)
- Evaluation pipeline or scoring
- Database schema

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- Chromium binary version downgrade (149→143) may produce slightly different PDF rendering (font hinting, spacing). Both versions use the same Chrome DevTools Protocol for `page.pdf()`.
- `puppeteer-core` downgrade (25→22) drops any new Puppeteer API features; this route only uses `launch`, `newPage`, `setContent`, `pdf` — all stable since v20.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: Confirmed — no evaluation logic changes, only download format rendering and dependency versions.
